title: Immune cell isolation from mouse brain and spleen for flow cytometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"rlogz","text":"This protocol outlines the procedures for terminal in vivo CD45 labeling, enzymatic dissociation of mouse brain tissue using collagenase D and DNase I, myelin removal via a Percoll gradient, and mechanical preparation of single-cell suspensions from the spleen. It also includes steps for red blood cell lysis in spleen lysate, antibody staining, fixation, and subsequent flow cytometric analysis of immune cells isolated from both brain and spleen tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":51,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"y44bg","text":"**Suggested gating workflow**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"smt5o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dxmmr","text":"I. Begin with FSC-SSC to exclude debris, then gate singlets.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ie2w4","text":"II. Exclude dead cells using viability dye.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wopwh","text":"III. Use the intravenously delivered CD45-BV711 signal to help distinguish vascular/circulating cells from brain-associated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"twwy1","text":"IV. Define myeloid and lymphoid compartments using CD45, CD11b, CD11c, Ly6C, Ly6G, F4/80, CD3e, CD4, CD8a, CD19, and NK1.1 as appropriate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ctgf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk1k8","text":"**Quality control**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"or5ej","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jfsjb","text":"I. Process all comparison groups in parallel.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9jrue","text":"II. Avoid prolonged delays between tissue dissociation and staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6oew8","text":"III. Inspect brain suspensions after Percoll cleanup as excessive residual myelin can degrade cytometer performance and data quality.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhkw2","text":"IV. Maintain consistent cell numbers and antibody incubation times across samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1tb3t","text":"- Prepare the glass pipette for trituration:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"83fff","text":"Melt the tip of the glass pipette to reduce the size of the hole using a Bunsen burner, rotating the pipette to avoid blocking the hole. Three different hole sizes will be created to allow the tissues to pass through progressively smaller holes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejelt","text":"- Prepare all digestion and staining reagents fresh.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sieuj","text":"- Pre-cool PBS- EDTA-FBS dissociation buffer, and collection tubes for spleen processing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0wpoe","text":"- Bring collagenase digestion reagents to working temperature immediately before brain dissociation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kxpf6","text":"- Plan acquisition order so that all groups are processed comparably across the experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"iyvx7","text":"Avoid harsh handling in appropriate buffer and/or temperature conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Anesthetize the mouse with an intraperitoneal injection of pentobarbital at 100 mg/kg .
Inject intravenously 3 µg BV711-conjugated anti-CD45 diluted in 50 µL of saline.
Allow 00:03:00 for intravascular labeling prior to terminal collection and cervical dislocation.
Tissue harvesting:
Digest minced brain tissue:
Add the pieces of tissue in a 15mL tube containing 4mL of collagenase D ( 2 mg/mL ) and DNase I ( 28 U/mL ) solution.
Stop enzyme activity by adding cold PBS containing 2 millimolar (mM) EDTA and 2 % (v/v) FBS.
Pass the dissociated tissue through a 100 µm strainer to remove undigested fragments
Centrifuge the homogenate at Room temperature for 200 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes and then for 300 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes discarding the supernatant carefully.
Plate cells: Perform staining at 4 °C in canonical round-bottom 96-well plates protected from light.
Viability stain:
Block nonspecific binding using Fc block solution:
Surface staining:
Wash using PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer.
Fix cells in 4% PFA (100µL in each well) for 00:10:00 .
Set controls: Prepare FMO controls and compensation controls using OneComp eBeads incubated with fluorescent antibodies.
Acquiring data:
Remove myelin:
Resuspend the pellet in 10 mL of 37 % (v/v) Percoll. This step have to be done with solution kept at Room temperature .
Aspirate the myelin layer and wash the cell pellet with PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer.
Centrifuge for 500 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with no brake.
Count recovered cells using a hemocytometer and proceed to antibody staining.
Mash the tissue through a 100 µm strainer using the thumb-rest of a syringe plunger.
Rapidly remove the brain and mince tissue with a scalpel.
Collect spleen from the same mouse and keep it in ice-cold PBS 1X.
Rinse the filter several times with PBS-EDTA-FBS dissociation buffer to maximize recovery.
Centrifuge homogenates at 1500 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes .
Resuspend splenocytes in 3 mL RBC lysis buffer and incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature .
Wash with PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer and centrifuge again for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Resuspend in 1 mL dissociation buffer, count cells, and reserve 2 million cells per spleen for antibody staining.

Every 00:15:00 , triturate with a glass pipette, keep cells in a separate tube and add fresh dissociation solution. Repeat 3 times and reduce the size of the glass pipette opening with each new repetition.
Put the tube for agitation in a water bath at 37 °C for a total time of 00:45:00 .
Distribute brain and spleen cells in different plates according to the experiment layout.
Centrifuge the plate at 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in fixable viability dye eFluor450 (diluted at 1:1000 in 1X PBS).
Incubate for 00:15:00 .
Wash, centrifuge the plate at 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds and discard the supernatant.
Resuspend cells in 50 µL of anti-CD16/32 Fc-blocking solution at 0.5 µg per million cells for 00:15:00 .
Add 50 µL of the antibody cocktail and incubate for 00:30:00 .
Centrifuge 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds , discard supernatant.
Analyze using FlowJo software
Select a flow cytometer with lasers and detectors compatible with the chosen fluorophores. Here, data were acquired using a BD LSRFortessa X-20.
Acquire samples using appropriate instrument settings (e.g., voltages and compensation).
creator: Amandine Even
